_meta.description: Emergency classification rules, trigger word lists, urgency tiers, and triage question flows for the inbound AI agent. Drive dispatch decisions from customer intake.

TIER_1_EVACUATE.description: Evacuate customer immediately. Call 911 and gas/utility company. Do NOT dispatch tech alone. Do NOT have customer use phones/light switches inside.
TIER_1_EVACUATE.response_script: This sounds like it could be a gas leak or carbon monoxide issue — please leave the building immediately without using light switches or your phone inside. Once you're safely outside, call 911 and your gas company. Do not go back inside until authorities clear the building. I'm going to flag this for immediate follow-up.

TIER_2_EMERGENCY_DISPATCH.description: Dispatch emergency tech immediately, 24/7. Do not put customer on hold.
phrases: sewage coming up through the drain
water heater making loud banging and leaking
TIER_2_EMERGENCY_DISPATCH.response_script: I'm going to get someone out to you right away — this sounds like an emergency. Can you shut off the main water supply while we get a technician dispatched? The main shutoff is usually near your water meter or where the main line enters the house.

TIER_3_SAME_DAY_URGENT.description: Schedule same-day appointment within 4 hours. Check technician availability immediately.
phrases: water coming up in the tub

conditional_phrases.condition: outdoor_temp_above_90f AND (elderly OR infant OR medical_equipment_in_home)

TIER_4_SCHEDULE.description: Schedule next available appointment. No urgency escalation needed.
phrases: want to replace old water heater
minor leak from toilet base occasionally
sometimes a little wet near the toilet
AC not as cold as it used to be

multi_fixture_rule.description: When multiple fixtures or drains are affected simultaneously, reclassify to main line job and escalate urgency.
detection_phrases: kitchen and bathroom both backing up
when I flush the toilet the sink gurgles
water comes up in the bathtub when I run the dishwasher
water comes up in the bathtub when I flush the toilet
multi_fixture_rule.action: reclassify job type from individual drain to MAIN_LINE, escalate to TIER_3_SAME_DAY_URGENT minimum

seasonal_adjustments.rule: Lower threshold for no-heat calls — treat as TIER_2_EMERGENCY if outdoor temp below 40°F or indoor temp falling below 55°F
Lower threshold for no-AC calls when household includes elderly (65+), infants under 2, or medical equipment — treat as TIER_2_EMERGENCY
Treat all frozen pipe reports as TIER_2_EMERGENCY even if no active break confirmed yet
Elevate all HVAC won't-turn-on calls from TIER_4 to TIER_3 urgency

plumbing.question: Is water actively running, spraying, or gushing right now?
plumbing.purpose: Detect active uncontrolled water release — escalate to TIER_2 if yes

plumbing.question: Do you smell anything like rotten eggs or sulfur?
plumbing.purpose: Gas leak detection — escalate to TIER_1 if yes

plumbing.question: Can you shut off the water? The shutoff is usually under the sink, behind the toilet, or near your water meter.
plumbing.purpose: Immediate mitigation — reduces damage while tech is dispatched

plumbing.question: Is sewage visible anywhere in the living areas of your home?
Are multiple drains affected, or is it just one?
plumbing.purpose: Multiple drains = main line involvement — reclassify job and elevate urgency

plumbing.question: Is there any water near electrical outlets, panels, or appliances?
plumbing.purpose: Electrocution risk — escalate to TIER_2 if yes

hvac.question: What is the current temperature inside your home right now?
hvac.purpose: Temperature-based urgency assessment for heating/cooling failures

hvac.question: What is the outdoor temperature where you are?
hvac.purpose: Context for winter no-heat and summer no-AC escalations

hvac.question: Do you smell anything burning, or do you smell gas?
Are there any sparks, smoke, or unusual sounds coming from the unit?
hvac.purpose: Fire risk or imminent mechanical failure

hvac.question: Is anyone in the home elderly, very young, or dependent on medical equipment?
hvac.purpose: Vulnerability assessment — lowers threshold for no-heat and no-AC escalation

hvac.question: When did it last work properly?
hvac.purpose: Duration of problem — helps differentiate acute failure from gradual degradation

clogged_toilet.question: Are other drains in the home also backing up or running slow?
clogged_toilet.purpose: If YES, reclassify as main line — not just a toilet clog

main_line.question: Is any sewage visible in the living areas of your home?

no_ac.question: What type of system do you have — central air, window unit, or mini-split?
What is the current indoor temperature?
Has the system made any unusual sounds before stopping?

no_heat.question: What is the outdoor temperature right now?
What does your thermostat display show?
Is there any burning smell or visible smoke?
Do you have any alternative heat source available?

water_heater.question: Is the unit gas or electric?
Is there any visible water leaking from the unit?
How old is the water heater approximately?

false_positive_guards.trigger_phrase: steam coming off the outdoor unit
false_positive_guards.action: Do not escalate. Explain to customer that this is normal operation.
false_positive_guards.response_script: That's actually completely normal! When your heat pump is in heating mode, it periodically runs a defrost cycle which causes steam or vapor to appear from the outdoor unit. It should clear up within a few minutes. Is the unit still heating your home properly?

false_positive_guards.context: first time turning on furnace for the season
false_positive_guards.action: Clarify with follow-up questions before escalating. If smell persists beyond 30 min or is accompanied by smoke, escalate.
clarifying_questions: Is this the first time you've turned on the heat this season?
Is there any visible smoke, or is it just a smell?
Has the smell gone away in the last few minutes?

false_positive_guards.trigger_phrase: water dripping outside from white pipe
false_positive_guards.context: AC condensate drain — normal in humid conditions
false_positive_guards.action: Clarify — if it's a white PVC pipe with slow drip from AC/air handler, this is normal. If it's the T&P valve on water heater, escalate.
clarifying_questions: Is the dripping pipe near your outdoor AC unit or near your water heater?
Is it a slow drip or steady flow?
